test_cases.description: Investigative podcast episode — Instagram and LinkedIn

input.content: Episode 6 of investigative documentary podcast — how a whistleblower protection law is used by corporations to suppress journalism

test_cases.notes: Instagram and LinkedIn versions are genuinely differentiated. Instagram opens with a hook, LinkedIn opens with insight. No generic filler phrases. Hashtags relevant.

test_cases.description: New magazine article — Twitter/X and TikTok

input.content: Feature article investigating how supermarket loyalty card data is sold to insurance companies — affecting premiums for people who buy alcohol, cigarettes, or certain medications
input.voice: Accessible, factual, with a sense of urgency

test_cases.notes: Twitter version works within character count. TikTok version is fast and punchy. Both find a different angle on the same story. TikTok version uses second-person address appropriately.

input.content: Feature documentary premiering at IDFA. Subject: climate grief counsellors who work with people experiencing psychological distress caused by climate change

test_cases.notes: Tone correctly avoids promotional register for a film account. Emotional hook appropriate for the subject. IDFA premiere mentioned as credential without being the lead.
